conditions: Image is very murky; only a vague, hazy outline of a single piling on the right is visible with no clear texture and no mid or far pilings discernible. This corresponds to extremely poor water clarity, under the 5 ft threshold.

Closest 4ft piling at right is sharp with clear texture. The 11ft piling behind it is visible but soft. The 14ft/30ft pilings toward the center are only broad hazy silhouettes with no texture, and the bottom is not visible. This corresponds best to the 15 ft guideline where the 14ft region is a faded silhouette.

Closest 4 ft piling at right edge is clear with some texture; mid-right (~11 ft) piling appears as a broad dark band but with very little detail and quickly fades. No distinct view of 14 ft or 30 ft pilings and bottom not visible. This corresponds to only the 11 ft piling being discernible beyond the nearest, so visibility is about 10 ft.

Closest 4ft piling at right edge is sharp with texture. The 11ft piling is visible with structure but somewhat hazy. Back-left/14ft piling is only seen as a faint vertical shadow with little detail, similar to the 25ft reference where distant pilings are silhouettes. No clear view of 30ft pilings or bottom. This matches conditions where the 14ft piling is hazy/faded, corresponding to about 15 ft visibility.

Right 4 ft piling is sharp with texture. The 11 ft piling is visible but slightly hazy. The 14 ft piling is present as a somewhat faded silhouette, and the far 30 ft pilings are not clearly distinguishable. This corresponds best to conditions where the 14 ft piling is hazy/faded.

Closest 4ft piling at right edge is sharp with clear texture. The ~11ft piling behind it is visible but quite hazy. The 14ft and 30ft pilings are essentially lost in the background haze with no clear silhouette. This aligns with conditions where only the 11ft piling is meaningfully visible beyond the nearest, indicating about 10 ft visibility.
